patterns.label: Azure DevOps Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: 30 distinct ADO notifications in 7 days (Tasks, Requirements, Bugs, Epics). PM is subscriber/mentioned recipient, not assignee — ideal triage candidate. Machine-parseable subject patterns (Task ###### - Eval), deterministic routing by type. Highest frequency signal in dataset. Auto ceiling 90-95%. Strong candidate for immediate automation.

patterns.label: Meeting-to-Loop Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: Cross-source chain head: Meeting scheduled → Loop Notes doc created → Decisions/action items captured → Follow-up continues asynchronously. Freq 4 in meetings, freq 4 in documents, same structural pattern. Stable template: Agenda / Meeting notes / Decisions / Open questions / Follow-up tasks. MPBI multiplier applies. Highest composite candidate.

patterns.label: Inbound Eval Strategy Guidance & Tooling Q&A
patterns.llmRationale: PM is sole DRI for 10+ request types: eval depth/timing/structure, metric selection (groundedness, tool_call_accuracy, Compare Meaning), CLI troubleshooting (CSV import/export, feature flags), golden eval design. 10 strategy Q&A + 5 tooling troubleshooting = 15 inbound pings in one week across Field Engineers, PMs, Designers. Expert Scaling Bottleneck (BSI escalating). T1 codifiable: scoping framework, metric selection rubric, CLI syntax guide. Highest value score due to breadth of audience and org-wide blocking impact.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Assembly (Multi-Format Duplication)
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly status required in done/next/blockers format, PLUS bi-weekly newsletter, PLUS Cape Day/LT slides. Teams asked multiple times to restate progress in different artifacts — SGI signal. Single source of truth → multi-format output is deterministic once template is set. Parallel creation duplication across PM, Program Managers, Team leads confirms SGI.

patterns.label: External Partner & Customer Eval Session Facilitation
patterns.llmRationale: 6 external partner sessions across 50+ distinct domains (Accenture, HCLTech, EY, KPMG, Avanade, Cognizant). EDII 5.0+ (50+ domains/week). 13 total hours — single largest time sink in dataset. Each session follows similar structure: eval design review → methodology → tooling walkthrough. Pre-session prep is highly automatable. EDII amplification applies (+3 valueScore).

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (SGI Gap)
patterns.llmRationale: 3 parallel eval deck versions independently created by PM, Program Manager, Field Engineer (TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks). Meetings explicitly flag multiple versions floating and request consolidation. Classic SGI with Parallel Creation Gap archetype. High automation ceiling: canonical template → role-specific variants.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Team Sync Operating Rhythm
patterns.llmRationale: 4 distinct weekly recurring meetings Monday/Tuesday: Evals Sync, Project Crucible v-Team, Manager Team Meeting, Solution Library Check-In. All extended through August with same participants and time slots. Pre-meeting agenda generation + post-meeting notes routing are both automatable. Calendar regularity = maximum automation ceiling.

patterns.label: Security & Compliance Alert Classification
patterns.llmRationale: 7 compliance alerts from Office365/Purview in 7 days (File Blocked, Administrator elevation, Loop workspace attention). Machine-parseable subjects, deterministic response: classify → route → acknowledge or escalate. PM is sole recipient. High cognitive interruption tax even at low time cost. Compliance/Governance Alert archetype (ceiling 85-91%).

patterns.label: Cross-Org Navigation & VOC Routing Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: PM serves as informal router between Field Engineers, MCS Analytics, and PG eval teams. 4 pings asking who owns X or requesting DRI contact for eval-related asks. PM functions as a living org chart. Automatable as a DRI directory skill with query→contact matching. Lower auto ceiling due to judgment in ambiguous routing, but high value as it unblocks multiple stakeholders.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams
patterns.llmRationale: 8 instances of sharing GitHub repos, ZIP files, YAML specs, markdown docs, Loop components via Teams. PM uses Teams as distribution hub for working artifacts. Partially automatable: standardized asset catalog → context-aware sharing template. Builder-User Prototyping archetype — PM simultaneously creating and distributing prototypes. Lower value due to small participant count.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page
patterns.llmRationale: 5 instances of '[Date] Help me prepare for my day' planning artifacts. PM creates structured daily planning page rather than starting from scratch — a Personal Productivity Ritual (NEW archetype, first observed this cycle). 2+ hrs/week of personal planning time. Highly automatable: pull calendar, pending action items, open emails, generate prioritized day brief. Lower value due to solo impact, but strong automation signal.

patterns.label: Immersive Training Event Cascade (Camp AIR)
patterns.llmRationale: Camp AIR: 2 full-day events (~9 hrs each) plus dozens of adjacent sessions. 70-80% of scheduled meeting time this week. CASCADE SOURCE — amplifies 4+ downstream patterns simultaneously. Apply 0.7x trend dampening to co-occurring patterns. Quarterly cadence predicted (next: June 2026). Artifact coordinator skill: pre-event prep bundle, standup template, EOD debrief generator, post-event synthesis doc.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers repeated eval strategy and tooling questions from field engineers, PMs, and customers. Covers eval depth/timing, metric selection (groundedness, tool_call_accuracy, Compare Meaning), CLI usage (CSV import/export, feature flags), and golden eval design. Eliminates the Expert Scaling Bottleneck where one PM fields 15+ inbound requests per week.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases are enough for an agent in early testing?
What metrics should I use for a grounding-focused Copilot eval?
The CSV import in the eval CLI is throwing an error — what am I doing wrong?
When in the agent lifecycle should evals start?
What's the difference between tool_call_accuracy and Compare Meaning?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates BSI-critical bottleneck. 15+ pings/week freed from single DRI. Scales eval expertise to 50+ external partner domains without PM bandwidth constraint.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: After any meeting, automatically drafts a Loop-formatted notes doc with Agenda recap, key Decisions, Action Items with owners, and Open Questions. Pulls from meeting transcript or summary. Eliminates the manual create-structure → fill-template → distribute loop.
triggerExamples: Generate meeting notes for today's Evals Sync
Extract action items from this Project Crucible meeting
Create a Loop doc from my 1:1 notes
Summarize decisions and next steps from this meeting
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head for the highest-value pattern cluster. 4+ meetings/week × recurring Loop doc creation. Unblocks downstream patterns: transcript-to-PPT, weekly status, team sync prep.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Processes Azure DevOps work-item notification emails by type (Task, Requirement, Bug, Epic, Feature) and routes them to the right triage action: watch-only, respond required, schedule follow-up, or escalate. Reduces 30+ emails/week to a prioritized action list.
triggerExamples: Triage my ADO notification emails from this week
Which of these ADO alerts need my response vs just awareness?
Sort my DevOps work-item emails by action type
Flag any ADO notifications that are blocking items
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Highest frequency signal (30/week). Pure triage with machine-parseable subject patterns. Near-full automation potential (90-95%). Immediate time recovery.

candidateSkills.name: partner-eval-enablement-pack-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates a pre-session enablement package for external partner or customer eval sessions: tailored agenda, relevant eval template, metric selection guide, CLI quick-start, and post-session follow-up template. Scales across 50+ partner domains without per-session manual prep.
triggerExamples: Prepare my eval session pack for an Accenture SI partner
Generate a pre-meeting eval brief for a new customer domain
Create the eval intro materials for tomorrow's KPMG session
What eval artifacts do I need for a first-time customer eval meeting?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 13 hours/week in external partner sessions. EDII 5.0+ (50+ unique domains). Each session prep is repeatable. Frees PM from per-engagement rebuild cycle.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Assembles the weekly status update from existing artifacts (ADO items, meeting notes, Loop docs) into the done/next/blockers format, then reformats for all required downstream artifacts: team email, bi-weekly newsletter, and leadership deck. Single input → multi-format output eliminates the SGI duplication gap.
triggerExamples: Generate my weekly status update from this week's work
Create the done/next/blockers summary for the team email
Turn my weekly notes into the newsletter format
Reformat my status update for the leadership slide
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Closes SGI gap where 3+ team members independently produce status artifacts. One canonical source → 3 outputs. Leadership visibility + team coordination multiplier.
